creator: Arjan.deVet@adv.iae.nl
text:
The following reply was made to PR ports/11239; it has been noted by GNATS.

From: Arjan de Vet <Arjan.deVet@adv.iae.nl>
To: Sheldon Hearn <sheldonh@uunet.co.za>
Cc: freebsd-gnats-submit@freebsd.org
Subject: Re: ports/11239: mutt 0.95.4 fails to detect sendmail location
Date: Wed, 26 May 1999 22:15:22 +0200

 Sheldon Hearn wrote:
 
 >Hi Arjan,
 >
 >Could you see whether David's changes to the mutt port (see the message
 >below) fix the problem for you?
 
 No :-(. The configure script hasn't changed that much.
 
 My PATH is
 /home/devet/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/X11R6/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/bin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/usr/sbin
 
 Running configure with a -x added after #!/bin/sh:
 
 [/usr/ports/mail/mutt] devet@adv> make
 ===>  Configuring for mutt-0.95.5
 + ac_help=
 + ac_default_prefix=/usr/local
 [...]
 + ac_aux_path_sendmail=/usr/sbin:/usr/lib
 + set dummy sendmail
 + ac_word=sendmail
 + echo -n checking for sendmail... 
 checking for sendmail... + echo configure:1151: checking for sendmail
 + echo ${ac_cv_path_SENDMAIL+set}
 + eval test "${ac_cv_path_SENDMAIL+set}" = set
 + test  = set
 + IFS= 	
 
 + ac_save_ifs= 	
 
 + IFS= 	
 :
 + test -z /home/devet/bin
 + test -f /home/devet/bin/sendmail
 + test -z /usr/local/bin
 + test -f /usr/local/bin/sendmail
 + test -z /usr/X11R6/bin
 + test -f /usr/X11R6/bin/sendmail
 + test -z /usr/local/sbin
 + test -f /usr/local/sbin/sendmail
 + test -z /bin
 + test -f /bin/sendmail
 + test -z /usr/bin
 + test -f /usr/bin/sendmail
 + test -z /sbin
 + test -f /sbin/sendmail
 + test -z /usr/sbin:/usr/sbin
 + test -f /usr/sbin /usr/sbin/sendmail
 test: syntax error
 + test -z /usr/lib
 + test -f /usr/lib/sendmail
 + IFS= 	
 
 + test -z 
 + ac_cv_path_SENDMAIL=no
 + SENDMAIL=no
 + test -n no
 + echo no
 no
 
 The problem occurs in this piece of code:
 
   ac_aux_path_sendmail=/usr/sbin:/usr/lib   
   [...]
   IFS="${IFS=   }"; ac_save_ifs="$IFS"; IFS="${IFS}:"
   for ac_dir in $PATH:$ac_aux_path_sendmail$ac_dummy; do
     test -z "$ac_dir" && ac_dir=.
     if test -f $ac_dir/$ac_word; then
       ac_cv_path_SENDMAIL="$ac_dir/$ac_word"
       break
     fi
   done
 
 The string $PATH:$ac_aux_path_sendmail$ac_dummy doesn't get split
 correctly on the colons for some reason.  This is with a FreeBSD
 3.2-RELEASE /bin/sh.
 
 Note: the configure script in mutt 0.95 is different and does detect
 sendmail correctly.
 
 Arjan
